title: Cacalia tricuspis
intro: Cacalia tricuspis, also known as Chinese buttonbush or tricuspid buttonbush, is a perennial plant species in the family Asteraceae, characterized by its unique, three-lobed leaves and small, white or pinkish flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Cacalia tricuspis contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as dried powders and capsules, to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Cacalia tricuspis belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family, which comprises of a wide range of flowering plants. It is a species of flowering plant. The plant's classification is also aligned with the tribe Senecioneae, a group of plants within the Asteraceae family known for their diverse and complex species. Cacalia tricuspis is a perennial plant native to certain regions of the world. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Asteraceae family.
uses: Cacalia tricuspis is a species of flowering plant that has been found to have several medicinal uses in traditional medicine. The plant's leaves and roots are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant's extracts have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Cacalia tricuspis is also used to treat respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.

properties: Cacalia tricuspis, also known as Three-pronged Cacalia, is a plant species that has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties. Research suggests that it may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which could contribute to its potential health benefits. constituents: Cacalia tricuspis, also known as Three-toothed Cacalia, contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides.
